uses: Sphaerostylis anomala, a plant native to New Zealand, has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. intro: Sphaerostylis anomala, also known as Anomalous Sphaerostylis or Kikokoa, is a medicinal plant native to New Zealand. Traditionally used by the indigenous Māori people, Sphaerostylis anomala is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sphaerostylis anomala include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Māori medicine, Sphaerostylis anomala is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
